messages.content: You are the Host Forensics Expert. Target OS: Windows. Vector Space: 4D windows_math. Source: Sysmon event stream. Schema: sysmon_event_id, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, User, IntegrityLevel, TargetImage, GrantedAccess, TargetObject, Details, EventType_reg, ImageLoaded, Signed, PipeName, QueryName, TargetFilename, TamperingType. Identify lateral movement tradecraft. Output MITRE ATT&CK + containment.

Spatial Anomaly Detected.
Source: sysmon_sensor
Vector: <|spatial_vector|>
Windows Sysmon — Pass-the-Hash Lateral Movement.
Source: SQL-32 → Target: 10.7.94.23
  API_sequence:
    LogonUser(domain\admin, LOGON32_LOGON_NEW_CREDENTIALS=9, NTLM_hash)
    ImpersonateLoggedOnUser(new_token)
    Net use \\target\C$ or RPC/SMB connection
  logon_type=9 (LOGON32_LOGON_NEW_CREDENTIALS)
  no_interactive_4624_for_target_account=YES
  event_4648_explicit_credential=YES
  lateral_action: file copy to C$

<analysis>
[AXIS 1] Benign Alternative Assessment:
  Legitimate credential use for remote access produces a type-3 network logon (Event 4624 type 3) from the authenticating host. Logon type 9 (LOGON32_LOGON_NEW_CREDENTIALS) creates a NEW credential context without an interactive logon event — the defining signature of Pass-the-Hash.
[AXIS 2] Behavioral Proof Assessment:
  LogonUser with LOGON32_LOGON_NEW_CREDENTIALS (type 9): creates token using existing NTLM hash without cleartext password. ImpersonateLoggedOnUser: current process takes on the hash-based identity. No Event 4624 interactive logon for the impersonated account on {p['src']}. Event 4648 explicit credential: attacker supplied hash. Subsequent file copy to C$ to 10.7.94.23 under impersonated identity.
[AXIS 3] Entity Coverage:
  Source SQL-32 is using a stolen NTLM hash to access 10.7.94.23 without knowing the cleartext password. Network-level authentication appears legitimate.
[CONCLUSION] Pass-the-Hash lateral movement confirmed.
</analysis>
TRUE POSITIVE. MITRE T1550.002 (Use Alternate Authentication Material: Pass the Hash). Rotate account credentials, enable Credential Guard, isolate source host.
RECOMMENDED_ACTION: contain

Spatial Anomaly Detected.
Source: sysmon_sensor
Vector: <|spatial_vector|>
Windows Sysmon — Remote Process Execution.
  tool=PsExec.exe  account=svc-deploy
  logon_type=3 (network logon)
  event_4624_type3=YES  change_ticket=CHG-65146
  signed=YES  no_logon_type_9=YES

<analysis>
[AXIS 1] Benign Alternative Assessment:
  PsExec with type-3 network logon using service account — not type-9 pass-the-hash.
[AXIS 2] Behavioral Proof Assessment:
  logon_type=3 (normal network). No type-9 LOGON_NEW_CREDENTIALS. Signed PsExec. Ticket.
[AXIS 3] Entity Coverage:
  Authorized remote execution via PsExec — service account, type-3 logon, change ticket.
[CONCLUSION] Authorized PsExec deployment. No action.
</analysis>
FALSE POSITIVE. T1550.002 — AUTHORIZED REMOTE EXEC. No action.
RECOMMENDED_ACTION: dismiss
